I switched that business model over to a coaching model for a period of time, and Jeff hired me to help coach one of his coaching programs, along with some other product launch managers that he trained during that event, so I was doing that for a number of years. So, for me, it was like when I heard about this program, it was like amazing, you know, like the next step to do it privately instead of... The model that I used and something that was taught to me was charging a retainer fee up front to hire me to manage the launch for them. And then we negotiated a percentage of the revenue on the back end. But it was real important to get that retainer up front because you'll find that a lot of clients, you know, they have no problem cutting you in on the revenue share on the back end, but you're going to do a lot of work for them for a decent period of time. It could be anywhere from 30 to 90 days or more before you'll see the results of that work because, you know, all the planning before the launch, until after the launch, you get the revenue, it's quite a long period of time that you definitely want to see some money up front to do that. And it's also a way for you to precision yourself in the market that, you know, in order for me to work with you, you know, you have to get on my schedule and here's what it costs. ## [27:33 - 30:34]

So my guess is your market is U.S. based? Yes. It's not a problem at all. In fact, you know, look, one of the things I never really find a bad time is to launch. I'm not sure there's definitely months that you want to stay away from and maybe even holidays like Thanksgiving here in the U.S. and things like that. But for me, product launch formula is event-based marketing and an event could be anything, right? So, in fact, one of the examples inside PLF is Jeff doing a tax sale during tax season. And what he did was he took the event of the fact that it is tax season and turned that event into, you know, the reason for this promotion. And you could do the same thing. It doesn't have to be because it's tax season, but, you know, any time you launch, and this is not just for you, but anybody else that's listening. And, you know, it could be because, you know, it's your 40th birthday. It's an event. So I don't think it's bad at all. If you're worried about it because you're afraid that because people are doing their taxes and they're going to have to, you know, pay out money in April, that the last place that they're going to, you know, pay with a credit card is to you because they're worried about paying their taxes. I don't know if that should be your number one worried. I mean, you know, if your marketing is right, if your transformation that you have is correct and your product's really good, people are going to buy it no matter when it is, even if they have to shell out money for the tax season. So, you know, I would spend more time being worried about my product that it's good enough and that my marketing message is right and that I have all that, you know, out there than I would that people aren't going to buy because it's tax season. That's very helpful. That's my opinion. Sure. ## [30:34 - 33:34]

And you start to see and you start to get a feel of what's happening. It might not be 100% on the money of what you think is happening.
